channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Harris will campaign in Texas to highlight state's abortion ban in a pitch to battleground voters
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris will head to reliably Republican Texas just 10 days before Election Day in an effort to refocus her campaign against former President Donald Trump on reproductive care, which Democrats see as a make-or-break issue this year.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Those early Zoom meetings got people fired up for Harris. Now they're trying to get them to vote
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When Joe Biden dropped out of the presidential race and Kamala Harris jumped in, a cascade of Zoom meetings with hundreds of thousands of participants popped up seemingly out of nowhere and helped propel her to the Democratic nomination.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FBI investigating classified docs leak of US intel on Israel planned strike on Iran
item.description: The FBI confirmed that the bureau is investigating the unauthorized release of top secret U.S. intelligence on Israel's planned counterattack on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Yellen says isolationism 'made America and the world worse off' in speech to global finance leaders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen is telling world financial leaders that the U.S. economy has grown stronger because the Biden administration rejected isolationism, offering a barely veiled criticism of former President Donald Trump’s policies two weeks before the U.S. election.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Metro Transit announces new Gold Line bus rapid transit route will be extended to Minneapolis
item.description: A new bus rapid transit route set to begin operating between Woodbury and St. Paul next spring will also be expanded to serve downtown Minneapolis in 2027, Metro Transit announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: How Project 2025's rightward vision became a flashpoint in this year's election
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — For the past year, Project 2025 has endured as a persistent force in the presidential election, its far-right proposals deployed by Democrats as shorthand for what Donald Trump would potentially do with a second term at the White House.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump leads Harris in Georgia 2 weeks from Election Day, poll finds
item.description: Former President Trump holds a slim lead over Vice President Kamala Harris in Georgia, one of the key states for winning the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris bets her policies can attract Latino voters while Trump touts his time as president to them
item.description: Both Kamala Harris and Donald Trump are highlighting their economic policies in their outreach to Latino voters with Election Day just two weeks away.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Harris bets her policies can attract Latino voters while Trump touts his time as president to them
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Both Kamala Harris and Donald Trump see economic policy as their best chance to win Latino voters. But their approaches are very different.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Legal experts question Harris' backing from donors with government ties
item.description: Vice President Harris raised eyebrows over potential conflicts of interest related to the support her presidential run has received from individuals and entities engaged in significant business before the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: St. Paul’s proposed tax hike to fund child care: 5 questions, answered
item.description: A St. Paul ballot question next month asks voters if they’ll authorize the city to levy property taxes to fund child care for low income families up to $20 million over 10 years. The mayor says he won’t implement the tax hike even if voters approve it. Here’s what to know.
item.dc:creator: Kyra Miles, MPR News

item.title: Harris touts growing up in middle class while pushing mandate most 'won't be able to afford': economist
item.description: Vice President Harris says she would grow the middle class if elected, but an economist says her administration's push for an EV mandate is not practical for middle-class citizens.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mideast conflict looms over US presidential race as Harris and Trump jostle for an edge
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Two weeks out from Election Day, the crisis in the Middle East is looming over the race for the White House, with one candidate struggling to find just the right words to navigate its difficult cross-currents and the other making bold pronouncements that the age-old conflict can qu
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The White House public tour has been upgraded so visitors can see, hear and touch more
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Jill Biden on Monday unveiled what she says is a reimagined White House public tour that will engage visitors' senses to teach them about the mansion's history and events that happened there. New to the tour is the Diplomatic Reception Room, which previously had been off-limits.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supervisor Leticia Perez under investigation for alleged use of county resources for political purposes
item.description: BAKERSFIELD, Calif. (KGET) -- An investigation is underway into Kern County Supervisor Leticia Perez after accusations that she used county resources for political purposes.
item.dc:creator: Jacqueline Gutierrez, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US service members abroad caught in the middle of overseas ballot battle raging between House Dems, GOP
item.description: Democrats say restrictions GOP lawmakers are seeking would disenfranchise thousands of U.S. service members and their spouses living overseas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump criticizes 'ugly' new NFL kickoff rule
item.description: "I hated seeing what the NFL did with the kickoff return,” Trump posted Sunday night on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, NewsNation

item.title: Convicted child-porn peddler asks court for non-prison punishment so he can advocate for trans daughter
item.description: The father of trans athlete Maëlle Jacques is seeking a non-prison sentence for child pornography charges, citing his advocacy for his trans daughter to play on girls sports teams.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arizona county supervisor takes plea deal after delaying 2022 election certification
item.description: A county official in rural Arizona pleaded guilty to a misdemeanor Monday after acknowledging delaying the vote canvass in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: From the desert to the Strip: How the election's fight over immigration may upend Nevada's economy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In the remote Nevada desert, the Baker Ranch couldn't survive without immigrant guest workers who come every year from Mexico.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats urge Biden to push Israel for press freedom in Gaza
item.description: Dozens of House Democrats are pressing the Biden administration to get tougher on Israel when it comes to press freedoms in Gaza, warning that a lack of media access in the war-torn territory has stifled any push for accountability surrounding military operations that have killed tens of thousands o
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Who is Lucifer 'Justin Case' Everylove? Meet the presidential candidate who is only on Utah's ballots
item.description: Who is Lucifer "Justin Case" Everylove? Here's how this unaffiliated presidential candidate made it onto the ballots in Utah.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, Fox 5

item.title: Trump bashes FEMA, NC governor as he tours Helene storm damage
item.description: Former President Trump on Monday attacked the federal response to Hurricane Helene by repeating debunked claims about funding and bashed North Carolina Gov. Roy Cooper (D) during a stop in storm-ravaged Asheville, N.C.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Central Park Five sue Trump for defamation over debate comments
item.description: The exonerated Central Park Five sued former President Donald Trump for defamation on Monday over his comments at the recent presidential debate about their wrongful convictions for rape and assault.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Should ID be required for voters? Nevada's Question 7 is discussed ahead of Election Day
item.description: As Election Day approaches, voters in Nevada will decide on a significant issue: whether the state should adopt voter ID requirements.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: GOP-leaning polls trigger questions about accuracy
item.description: The prevalence of Republican-leaning polls in recent weeks is raising questions about their effect on the overall polling average as the presidential race between Vice President Harris and former President Trump tightens even further.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: White House proposes insurers cover cost of over-the-counter birth control
item.description: The White House on Monday announced a proposal that would require insurers to cover the cost of forms of contraception like over-the-counter birth control pills and condoms under the Affordable Care Act.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court turns away challenge to consumer safety agency’s structure
item.description: The Supreme Court announced Monday it won’t take up a case that could have delivered another blow to federal agencies. Two conservative groups sought to challenge the structure of the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC), which oversees making sure the country’s consumer products are safe.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court won’t revive Michael Cohen retaliation suit against Trump
item.description: The Supreme Court will not wade into Michael Cohen’s long-waged war against former President Trump, declining Monday to revive his lawsuit seeking damages for retaliation during his prison sentence.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Early voting opens Monday throughout Chicago, expands to more locations in suburbs
item.description: CHICAGO — Early voting for the Nov. 5 General Election opens Monday morning throughout the city and expands to more locations around Chicagoland. Early voting in all 50 of Chicago's wards opens at 9 a.m. Monday. Any voter in the city can vote at an early voting site, no matter where they live.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: How does abortion translate? Ballot measures challenge interpreters
item.description: Vague or technical terms can be challenging, even more so when it comes to Indigenous languages that have only limited written dictionaries.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Arnold Palmer's daughter reacts to Donald Trump's references to her father
item.description: One of the late golf legend Arnold Palmer's daughters calls Donald Trump's references to her father's genitalia “a poor choice of approaches" to honoring his memory, adding that she wasn't upset by the remarks. “There’s nothing much to say.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about Republican challenges to overseas and military voting
item.description: In three swing states, GOP groups are suing to challenge the ballots cast by U.S. citizens living abroad, including military members. Many voting experts say these cases are likely to go nowhere.
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang, MPR News

item.title: Trump expresses frustration about calls to deploy Haley
item.description: “But let me just tell you: Nikki Haley and I fought, and I beat her by 50, 60, 90 points. I beat her in her own state. I beat Nikki badly.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former Minnesota Congressman Rick Nolan dies at age 80
item.description: Former U.S. Rep. Rick Nolan, who represented two of Minnesota’s congressional districts in Washington more than 30 years apart, has died at the age of 80.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff and Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: Florida elections officials seek flexibility on voting rules after hurricanes
item.description: Florida's recent hurricanes could make it difficult for voters in affected areas to cast their ballots in the upcoming election unless Gov. Ron DeSantis waives and modifies some rules, county election supervisors said in a letter to state officials.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
